title: Verbose Error Messages
description: Learn how detailed error messages can unintentionally expose sensitive information that helps attackers understand and target a web application.

attack_stages.title: Application Error
attack_stages.description: An unexpected error occurs while the website processes a user's request.

attack_stages.title: Information Disclosure
attack_stages.description: The website displays detailed technical information about the application.

attack_stages.title: Reconnaissance
attack_stages.description: The attacker studies the error message to learn about the application's technologies and internal structure.

attack_stages.title: Attack Planning
attack_stages.description: The attacker uses the disclosed information to identify potential weaknesses.

attack_stages.title: Targeted Attack
attack_stages.description: The attacker launches more focused attacks using the information gathered.

prevention_points.title: Display Generic Error Messages
prevention_points.description: Show simple, user-friendly messages instead of technical details.

prevention_points.title: Log Errors Internally
prevention_points.description: Store detailed error information in secure server logs for administrators.

prevention_points.title: Hide Sensitive Information
prevention_points.description: Never expose file paths, software versions, database details, or stack traces to users.

prevention_points.title: Review Application Logs
prevention_points.description: Monitor recurring errors to identify and fix application issues before attackers exploit them.

nodes.title: Scenario
nodes.text: A company website encounters an unexpected error while processing a user's request.

nodes.title: What Are Error Messages?
nodes.text: When something goes wrong, web applications display error messages. These messages should help users understand that an error occurred without revealing technical details about the system.

nodes.title: Verbose Error Messages
nodes.text: Instead of showing a simple message, the website displays detailed technical information such as software versions, database errors, file paths, or application configuration details.

nodes.title: Attacker Goal
nodes.text: The attacker wants to learn how the website is built by analyzing the information exposed in its error messages.

nodes.title: Why are detailed error messages dangerous?

choices.text: They reveal technical information that helps attackers plan future attacks.
They automatically protect the website from attacks.
They prevent users from accessing the website.

nodes.title: Try Again
nodes.text: Verbose error messages do not directly compromise the system, but they provide valuable information that attackers can use during reconnaissance.

nodes.title: Reconnaissance Successful
nodes.text: The attacker gathers information such as application frameworks, software versions, database names, file locations, or configuration details. This information helps them identify potential vulnerabilities to target.

nodes.title: Impact
nodes.text: Although verbose error messages do not directly compromise the application, they significantly increase the likelihood of successful attacks by providing attackers with valuable intelligence about the system.

nodes.title: How to Prevent This
nodes.text: Display generic error messages to users, log detailed errors internally for administrators, remove sensitive technical information from public error pages, and regularly review application logs to identify recurring issues.

nodes.title: Attack Replay
nodes.text: Review how detailed error messages unintentionally revealed information that allowed an attacker to better understand and target the web application.

nodes.title: Scenario Complete
nodes.text: Verbose error messages assist attackers during the reconnaissance phase by exposing technical details about an application. Generic user-facing errors and secure internal logging help reduce this risk.
